whatThisIs: Look up an industrial or laboratory chemical in the NIOSH Pocket Guide to Chemical Hazards. Sophie Well returns the CAS number, the recommended exposure limit (REL) and the OSHA permissible exposure limit (PEL), the immediately-dangerous-to-life-or-health (IDLH) value, the physical properties (vapor pressure, flash point, boiling point), the target-organ list, and the recommended respirator and personal-protective-equipment selections for exposure scenarios up to the IDLH.
whenToUse: Use this when a clinician, industrial hygienist, or occupational medicine consultant is workup an inhalational or dermal exposure to an identified industrial chemical, when a CHEMPACK or hazmat team needs the IDLH-based respirator-selection criteria, or when an emergency department is trying to anticipate target-organ toxicity for a still-symptomatic worker. The NIOSH Pocket Guide is a reference for known chemicals at industrial exposure levels - not for unknown intoxications or for medication overdoses (use tox-levels for therapeutic-drug ranges).
